about.description:
Universal silent installer for programs and system tweaks.

Cross-platform: Windows (.exe, .msi, .bat, .cmd, .reg, PowerShell, winget, choco) and Linux (apt/dpkg, flatpak, snap, .deb, .AppImage, bash).
program_editor.title: Edit Program
program_editor.name: Name:
program_editor.error_empty_fields: Name and install command are required.
menu.add_program_guide.tooltip: How to add a program to the catalog
guide.add_program.title: Guide: adding programs
guide.add_program.body:
How to add a program to the MInstAll catalog.

1. Menu "File -> Add program" (or right-click a category -> "Add").
   Fill in: Name, Command (cmd), optionally URL, SHA-256, category,
   dependencies, pre/post commands, detection rules (detect).

2. Entry structure in programs.json:
   {
     "name": "Name",
     "cmd": "software\Folder\install.exe /S",
     "desc": "Description",
     "icon": "icons/system.png",
     "version": "1.0",
     "os": "windows",
     "url": "https://example.com/setup.exe",
     "sha256": "abcdef...",
     "depends_on": ["Other program"],
     "pre_cmd": "...",
     "post_cmd": "...",
     "uninstall_cmd": "...",
     "retry": 1,
     "timeout": 900,
     "detect": {
       "registry_name": "...",
       "path": "%PROGRAMFILES%\App\app.exe",
       "min_version": "1.0",
       "always_runnable": false,
       "net_framework_release": 528040
     }
   }

3. Silent flags by extension:
   .exe -> /S (NSIS), /VERYSILENT (InnoSetup)
   .msi -> msiexec /i ... /qn /norestart
   .deb -> apt-get install -y
   .sh / .bash -> bash
   .AppImage -> run directly

4. Cross-platform: set "os": "windows" or "os": "linux" to hide the
   entry on the other OS. Path separators are normalized automatically
   (\ and / are interchangeable).

5. Security: the command is checked against shell metacharacters
   (& | ; $ ` > < ^) and by extension. Allowed bare commands:
   winget, choco, apt, dpkg, snap, flatpak; allowed extensions:
   .exe .msi .bat .cmd .ps1 .reg .sh .bash .deb .appimage.

6. Saving: "File -> Save catalog" writes changes to programs.json.
   The software/ folder is scanned automatically
   (Tools -> Rescan).
